quote: A problem well stated is a problem half-solved.
through_line: The same thread runs through a classroom, a DMO’s content strategy, and a question about whether insects are conscious: when you break a problem down carefully enough, something surprising emerges. Computational thinking isn’t just a skill — it’s a posture toward complexity.

articles.title: New Skills and AI Are Reshaping the Future of Work
articles.pitch: The IMF’s emerging picture of AI’s labor market impact is nuanced and more urgent than most headlines capture: complementary skills matter enormously, and those who lack them are exposed in ways the top of the distribution rarely sees. Short, evidence-dense, and worth the time.

articles.title: Generative Engine Optimization 101: How to Optimize Content that Gets Noticed with Generative AI
articles.pitch: GEO — Generative Engine Optimization — is what SEO is becoming: structuring content so AI systems surface it, not just crawlers. Madden Media’s primer explains the mechanics and offers DMOs a starting point for thinking about how their content strategy needs to change.

articles.title: GEO for DMOs: how Generative Engine Optimization changes the SEO game
articles.pitch: Simpleview’s take on GEO goes deeper into the DMO-specific playbook — what destination content needs to do to appear in AI-generated answers, and how the shift from keyword optimization to authority and structured clarity changes the work.

articles.title: Four computational thinking strategies for building problem-solving skills across the curriculum
articles.pitch: Decomposition, pattern recognition, abstraction, algorithmic design — these four strategies show how computational thinking integrates across all subjects, not just tech class. Project Tomorrow’s teachers demonstrate it works as a classroom language, not just a coding exercise.

articles.title: If AIs can feel pain, what is our responsibility towards them?
articles.pitch: As AI systems grow more sophisticated, the question of moral patienthood becomes harder to dismiss. This Aeon essay takes the possibility of machine suffering seriously — not to resolve it, but to examine what ethical frameworks we’d need if the answer turned out to be yes.

articles.title: Impermanence
articles.pitch: Fronsdal’s brief essay on impermanence does what dharma writing at its best does: it moves from doctrine to practice to the felt sense of what it means to actually live alongside change. A steady companion for a midweek practice.

articles.title: Insects and Other Animals Have Consciousness, Experts Declare
articles.pitch: A group of leading neuroscientists signed the New York Declaration on Animal Consciousness — a formal acknowledgment that the capacity for experience extends far beyond mammals. Quanta captures the significance of the moment: science cautiously catching up to what many traditions have always held.
